violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as shrimp and shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as prepared pork and vegetables) in the 2-door reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Raw animal foods were moved during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

Birria sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Per PIC, food item was prepared the previous day at 8pm and was observed at temperatures ranging from 48-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, birria sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in plastic containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling methods were reviewed and guidance was provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

The door to the storage room, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

The door to the storage room, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
A proper air gap is lacking at the ice machine. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
The doors to the toilet rooms are not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
